text.title: Shared Task Store Invariants
text.summary: The store keyed by task_id is the source of truth; agents pull from it on receipt and write back on completion.

section.title: Store invariants
section.p: The store can be a filesystem queue, a Redis hash, a database row — the choice doesn't matter as long as four invariants hold.

rule.statement: The receiving agent MUST load task state from the shared store on first action — not from the handoff payload. The payload's decision_metadata is for routing decisions only, not for reconstructing inputs.
rule.rationale: Single source of truth. If the metadata and the store disagree, the store wins; otherwise duplication causes drift between what the agent thinks the task is and what it actually is.

rule.statement: The store MUST expose at least three operations: get(task_id) → state, append_event(task_id, event), set_status(task_id, status). State updates MUST be append-only events, never overwrites of prior fields, so a downstream agent or a re-run can replay history.
rule.rationale: Append-only state lets a failed handoff be retried without losing the prior agent's output; overwrite stores destroy evidence on retry.

example.code: # NERO mesh — files-as-queue /srv/nero/queue/t_8821.json # task state /srv/nero/events/t_8821.jsonl # append-only event log
example.note: Worker on receipt: read t_8821.json; on completion: append to events/t_8821.jsonl; supervisor: ls events/ for status.

example.code: # Worker overwrites the task state on completion store.put(task_id, worker_output) # prior input is gone
example.note: If a downstream editor agent fails and we want to retry, the original input is lost.

antipattern.description: Using the handoff payload as the only source of task state, with no shared store at all.
antipattern.reason: Re-runs and audit trails become impossible; the system has no memory between agents besides what was passed in the last hop.

section.reference: OpenAI Agents SDK — handoff with shared task context.
Anthropic multi-agent research system — task state via shared store.
